2603.17198.title: Abstraction as a Memory-Efficient Inductive Bias for Continual Learning
2603.17198.paper_summary: The real world is non-stationary and infinitely complex, requiring intelligent agents to learn continually without the prohibitive cost of retraining from scratch. While online continual learning offers a framework for this setting, learning new information often interferes with previously acquired knowledge, causes forgetting and degraded generalization. To address this, we propose Abstraction-Augmented Training (AAT), a loss-level modification encouraging models to capture the latent relational structure shared across examples. By jointly optimizing over concrete instances and their abstract representations, AAT introduces a memory-efficient inductive bias that stabilizes learning in strictly online data streams, eliminating the need for a replay buffer. To capture the multi-faceted nature of abstraction, we introduce and evaluate AAT on two benchmarks: a controlled relational dataset where abstraction is realized through entity masking, and a narrative dataset where abstraction is expressed through shared proverbs. Our results show that AAT achieves performance comparable to or exceeding strong experience replay (ER) baselines, despite requiring zero additional memory and only minimal changes to the training objective. This work highlights structural abstraction as a powerful, memory-free alternative to ER.
2603.17198.paper_summary_zh: 現實世界是非穩定且無限複雜的，要求智能代理不斷學習，而不需承擔從頭開始重新訓練的高昂成本。雖然在線持續學習為這種情境提供了一個框架，但學習新信息往往會干擾先前獲得的知識，導致遺忘和泛化能力下降。為了解決這個問題，我們提出了抽象增強訓練（AAT），這是一種損失級別的修改，鼓勵模型捕捉跨範例共享的潛在關係結構。通過對具體實例及其抽象表徵進行聯合優化，AAT 引入了一種記憶高效的歸納偏見，穩定了在嚴格在線數據流中的學習，消除了對重放緩衝區的需求。為了捕捉抽象的多面性，我們在兩個基準上引入並評估了 AAT：一個受控的關係數據集，其中通過實體掩蔽實現抽象，以及一個敘事數據集，其中通過共享諺語表達抽象。我們的結果顯示，儘管 AAT 需要零額外內存並且僅對訓練目標進行最小更改，但其性能可與強大的經驗重放（ER）基準相媲美或超越。這項工作突顯了結構抽象作為一種強大且無需記憶的替代方案，取代了 ER。
2603.17198.author: Elnaz Rahmati et.al.
2603.17198.authors: Elnaz Rahmati, Nona Ghazizadeh, Zhivar Sourati, Nina Rouhani, Morteza Dehghani
